description: Dot product of two vectors or points. Works for both 2D and 3D. Can be used as a standalone function with vector literals or vector variables.
utility: Calculate the dot product of two vectors

arguments.name: vectorA
arguments.description: First vector (vector literal {x,y}, point tuple, or vector variable)

arguments.name: vectorB
arguments.description: Second vector (vector literal {x,y}, point tuple, or vector variable)

alternates.description: Dot product of two 2D vector literals
Dot product of two 3D vector literals
Dot product of two named vector variables

alternates.variant: Dot product via p() for graph vectors
alternates.description: Dot product of two graph vector expressions using the p() property accessor

examples.scenario: Dot product of graph vectors via p()
examples.expression:
G = g2d(at(10, 10), size(25), range(-6, 6), type(geometry))
A = vector(G, point(G, 0, 0), point(G, 3, 0))
B = vector(G, point(G, 0, 0), point(G, 0, 4))
d = p(A, B, dot)
